app.title: one PBX
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Billing, PBX tools, and telephony signals in one place—the portal speaks the language of calls.
dashboard.telephonySubtitle: Call volume, mix, quality (ASR) and recent legs for the selected organization.
dashboard.orgTelephonyHint: Below you will see KPIs, an hourly profile, and recent inbound/outbound legs for the selected tenant.
dashboard.telephonyFail: Could not load telephony metrics. Try again or check the API.
dashboard.orgWelcomeBody: Use the sidebar to manage PBX tools, integrations, and the calls workspace for your organization.
orgs.createSubtitle: Create a new company on the platform.
orgDetail.cdrHint: Asterisk MySQL credentials. The accountcode field filters calls per company.
orgs.title: Organizations
orgs.subtitle: Search, sort, and paginate tenants.
orgs.forbidden: You do not have access to this list.
profile.title: My profile
profile.subtitle: Your name and photo URL appear in the header menu.
profile.avatarHint: Use a square image URL (HTTPS). Shown as a circle in the top bar.
profile.avatarUrlInvalid: Must be empty or start with http:// or https://
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Branding, domains, and portal identity.
settings.logoHint: Used in the tenant picker and on the login screen when this host matches the organization.
settings.previewBody: This card uses the same CSS variables as the login screen.
settings.domainHint: Point DNS to this platform, then verify ownership. If the portal host differs from the PBX host, PBX embeds may require a separate login.
settings.pickOrg: Select an organization to edit settings.
spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.subtitle: Logical sites or branches under this organization.
spaces.name: Name
spaces.confirmDelete: Delete this space? Extensions or data tied to it may break.
webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.subtitle: Get real-time HTTP notifications when PBX events occur.
webhooks.createHint: Choose which events to listen for and configure the endpoint that will receive notifications.
webhooks.pickOrg: Select an organization to manage webhooks.
extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.subtitle: Directory synced or created in the portal.
extensions.onlyPortalHint: Only portal-created extensions can be edited here.
extensions.pickOrg: Select an organization to list extensions.
pbxScreen.pickOrg: Select an organization to use PBX tools.
pbxScreen.consoleSubtitle: Operational snapshot for this tenant: traffic, extensions, and shortcuts into each PBX workspace.
pbxScreen.consoleBullet1: Telephony KPIs and recent legs are read from the same `/metrics/telephony-overview` service used on the dashboard.
pbxScreen.consoleBullet2: Extensions are loaded from the portal API so directory changes stay in sync.
pbxScreen.consoleBullet3: Each PBX menu item below is a first-class React screen—no iframe required for day-to-day monitoring.
pbxScreen.vmSubtitle: Per-mailbox new message counts, recorded minutes and last caller.
pbxScreen.campSubtitle: Campaign pacing, CPS caps, and progress—surface for supervisors before agents dial.
pbxScreen.trunkSubtitle: Carrier trunks with codecs, channel budgets, and synthetic latency for NOC-style triage.
pbxScreen.didSubtitle: DIDs mapped to queues, IVRs, or extensions with timezone context.
pbxScreen.featSubtitle: Star codes and toggles operators expect—values are illustrative until AMI writebacks land.
pbxScreen.isabelCallsSubtitle: Live mix and recent PSTN legs for this tenant—same payload as the portal Calls workspace.
pbxScreen.rptSubtitle: Queue SLA, agent occupancy, and trunk utilization packs scoped to telephony operators.
pbxScreen.rptQueuesDesc: ASA, abandon %, and service level by skill group.
pbxScreen.rptAgentsDesc: Login sessions, pause reasons, and AHT estimates.
pbxScreen.rptTrunksDesc: Peak concurrent channels vs provisioned limits.
pbxScreen.setSubtitle: Music on hold, alert profiles, and quiet hours—portal-native controls layered on top of your PBX.
pbxScreen.setMohBody: Brand-safe playlists per queue with seasonal overrides.
pbxScreen.setAlertsBody: Webhook + email when abandon spikes exceed thresholds.
pbxScreen.setQuietBody: Suppress dialer retries during local night windows.
pbxScreen.setNoteBody: These toggles are scoped to the portal shell. Global PBX parameters still live in the classic UI if you need low-level Asterisk edits.
pbx.native.description: Manage PBX settings, integrations and advanced features directly from here.
pbx.native.footer: Opens in a new browser tab. Same hostname as PBX keeps your PBX session.
pbx.native.noOrg: Select an organization in the sidebar to contextualize PBX links.
pbx.section.dash: Snapshot of trunks, queues, and registrations. Operators still access the PBX for deep edits—this page keeps context inside the portal.
pbx.section.voicemail: Inbox depth, greeting slots, and MWI hints. Tie voicemail events to webhooks when you need CRM follow-up.
pbx.section.campaigns: Dialer pacing and abandon targets live here. Link outbound lists to compliance rules before agents press start.
pbx.section.calls: Live channels and last-changed dial peers. Pair this view with the portal Calls page for CDR-style drill-down.
pbx.section.termination: Carrier IPs, codecs, and max channels. Use it when debugging one-way audio or SIP OPTIONS health.
pbx.section.inbound: DID inventory and time conditions. Shows how calls land before they hit queues or IVR scripts.
pbx.section.features: Feature codes, parking, and follow-me toggles. Safe sandbox for toggling behavior without losing PBX defaults.
pbx.section.reports: Queue SLA and agent occupancy rollups. Export paths mirror PBX reports while staying inside your tenant shell.
pbx.section.settings: Branding and module access for PBX operators. Portal SSO and PBX classic UI can coexist on the same host.
calls.title: Calls
calls.subtitle: Direction mix, answer rate, ASR, and recent legs—same metrics feed dashboards and automations.
calls.pickOrg: Select an organization to load telephony metrics.
users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Portal identities and roles tied to organizations.
users.forbidden: You do not have access to this directory.
security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Access policies, SIP edge posture and event audit trail.
security.cardMfaBody: Stage TOTP for admins first, then org operators. Recovery codes stay offline.
security.cardApiBody: Rotate keys quarterly; scope webhooks per space to blast-radius limits.
security.cardSipBody: TLS + SRTP where supported; geo-fencing trunk IPs at the firewall.
reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: CDR, queues, billing exports, and SLA packs—stubs today, CSV scheduling next.
reports.cdrDesc: Filter by trunk, disposition and agent leg with detailed granularity.
reports.queuesDesc: Answered vs abandoned, ASA, and callback promises per skill group.
reports.billingDesc: Usage-based rollups aligned to your commercial model.
reports.slaDesc: Monthly attestations for enterprise customers with PDF cover pages.
diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: MySQL-backed counters for operators—safe read-only snapshot.
dashboard.platformSectionSubtitle: Platform totals, aggregated channel caps, and host disk. Bucket chart appears when PBX MySQL CDR is configured.
dashboard.simultaneousVolumeHint: With CDR: calls per time bucket (approximation; not AMI).
dashboard.demoBannerBody: No CDR source configured for this organization. Set the PBX URL in the organization settings to see real call data.
dashboard.demoNoCalls: Set up the CDR integration to see real calls here.
calls.onlineSubtitle: Per company: recent estimate vs channel cap.
calls.historySubtitle: Queries PBX MySQL when configured on the organization or via environment variables.
calls.historyCdrMissing: CDR not configured. Please set the CDR MySQL credentials in the company settings.
security.blocklistSubtitle: Manually blocked IPs/ports (stored in the portal DB).
security.trustlistSubtitle: Trusted IPs that should not be auto-blocked.
security.autoSubtitle: Parameters persisted here (applying to a real firewall is outside this portal).
reports.cdrExportDesc: Download up to 50k rows for the selected range (requires MySQL CDR).
page.placeholderBody: This screen is a placeholder until native data or PBX embed wiring is enabled.
page.placeholderBullet1: Roadmap: native tables, filters, and actions in the portal.
page.placeholderBullet2: Integrations and webhooks already live in other sections.
page.placeholderBullet3: PBX screens use the new native layout with an external PBX link.
page.forbiddenBody: You do not have permission to view this area.
whatsapp.title: WhatsApp
whatsapp.subtitle: Business messaging alongside voice—templates, opt-in and call-flow handoffs.
whatsapp.cardChannelBody: WhatsApp gateways registered and active for this organization.
whatsapp.cardTemplatesBody: Marketing vs utility categories with locale fallbacks.
whatsapp.cardPolicyBody: Opt-in receipts and 24h session windows enforced before send.
whatsapp.flowBody: Route WhatsApp replies into the same graph you use for PSTN legs—shared variables and actions.
whatsapp.bullet1: Inbound WhatsApp can enqueue agents with the same music-on-hold profile as voice.
whatsapp.bullet2: Template sends respect quiet hours per tenant timezone.
whatsapp.bullet3: Delivery receipts can fan out through webhooks alongside SIP events.
events.callInboundStartedDesc: Fired when a call enters the system
events.callOutboundStartedDesc: Fired when the system originates a call
events.callEndedDesc: Any call that finished (answered or not)
events.callMissedDesc: Call that was not answered and timed out or was hung up
events.extRegisteredDesc: A SIP extension completed registration successfully
events.extUnregisteredDesc: A SIP extension lost its registration
events.voicemailReceivedDesc: A user received a new voicemail message
events.trunkStatusChangedDesc: A SIP trunk came online or went offline
pbx.campaignListBody: Manage active and paused call campaigns in this organization.
pbx.campaignSchedulesBody: Configure the schedules used by campaigns.
pbx.campaignAudioBody: Manage audio files used in campaigns.
pbx.campaignRatingsBody: Manage contact ratings and reluctance rules.
pbx.peopleListBody: Manage people linked to extensions in this organization.
pbx.extensionGroupsBody: Manage groups of extensions for routing and policies.
pbx.teamsBody: Organize people into teams for reporting and management.
pbx.extensionListBody: Manage SIP extensions registered in this organization.
pbx.recordingsBody: Browse and download call recordings for this organization.
pbx.callingPlanBody: Configure outbound calling plans and routing rules.
pbx.trunksBody: Manage SIP trunks connected to this organization.
pbx.audioFilesBody: Manage audio prompts and music-on-hold files.
pbx.queuesBody: Configure call queues and agent assignment rules.
pbx.callFlowsBody: Design inbound call routing flows with drag-and-drop.
pbx.holdGroupBody: Manage hold music groups for queues and transfers.
pbx.internalNumbersBody: Manage the internal number plan for this organization.
pbx.conferenceRoomsBody: Create and manage conference rooms for multi-party calls.
pbx.urasBody: Configure Interactive Voice Response menus for this organization.
pbx.reportQueuesBody: View queue performance metrics and agent statistics.
pbx.reportOperationsBody: Consolidated operational reports for this organization.
pbx.reportDetailBody: Detailed breakdown of call operations and agent activity.
pbx.reportExportsBody: Export reports in CSV or Excel format.
pbx.reportAsrBody: Answer Seizure Ratio report showing call quality metrics.
pbx.reportAgentsBody: Track operator login, logout, pause, and call actions.
pbx.reportCampaignBody: Monitor campaign call queue progress and results.
pbx.costCenterBody: Manage cost centers for call accounting.
pbx.generalSettingsBody: Configure general PBX settings for this organization.
pbx.systemInfoBody: View PBX system information and health status.
pbx.holidaysBody: Manage the list of holidays for time-based routing rules.
pbx.pauseListBody: Manage pause types available to agents.

holiday.name: Name

settings.editHint: To change these settings, go to the organization configuration.
settings.adminOnly: This information is only available to platform administrators.

report.empty: No calls found for the selected period.
pbx.callFlowsPortalHint: Call flows are managed directly in the portal flow builder.
pbx.urasPortalHint: URAs/IVRs are built using the portal call flow builder.

whatsapp.fieldTemplatePlaceholder: e.g. Hello {{name}}, your call has been scheduled.
whatsapp.configBody: Gateway connected and active. Edit or disconnect below.
whatsapp.confirmDisconnect: Disconnect this gateway? The WhatsApp integration will become inactive.
